decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling UrzaTron (Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Tower) to generate huge amounts of mana and cast powerful threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Emrakul, the Promised End. It utilizes control elements like Chalice of the Void, Thought-Knot Seer, and All Is Dust to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Glaring Fleshraker and Kozilek's Command provide additional creature removal, while cards like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Trinisphere offer further control and disruption. The goal is to outvalue and outlast the opponent with a combination of powerful threats and control elements.

This deck focuses on using Eldrazi creatures like World Breaker and Emrakul, the Promised End as threats while ramping up mana with Utopia Sprawl and Eldrazi Temple. Control elements like Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble help to manage the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes cards like Devourer of Destiny and Herigast, Erupting Nullkite for additional pressure. Overall, the strategy is to overpower the opponent with big creatures and efficient removal spells, while using ramp to accelerate into powerful plays.

This Boros Ponza Modern deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources through land destruction and removal spells while using creatures with flash and creature spells with flashback for added versatility. The deck also utilizes Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion for additional synergy with its creature-focused strategy. By disrupting the opponent's mana base and removing threats, this deck aims to establish board control and secure victory through a mix of direct damage and creature pressure.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble a combination of cards that synergize together to create a powerful board state. The deck utilizes cards like Basking Broodscale, Blade of the Bloodchief, and Glaring Fleshraker to generate advantage and control the game. With the help of cards like Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga, the deck can find pieces of the combo efficiently. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Soul-Guide Lantern and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and abilities that allow for repeated enters-the-battlefield effects through blinking, enabling strong tempo plays and disruption. By constantly bouncing and replaying creatures like Witch Enchanter and Guide of Souls with cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with value and disruption. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Prismatic Ending and Spell Pierce to handle threats and protect its game plan.

The deck is focused on assembling a powerful combo involving Goblin Charbelcher and Hydroelectric Specimen to deal massive damage to the opponent. It heavily relies on countermagic to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes card draw and tutoring effects to help find key cards quickly. The goal is to efficiently control the game until the combo can be executed, without relying on traditional land cards for mana.

The deck aims to quickly reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance, while also utilizing instant-speed interaction like Solitude and Force of Negation to protect the combo. It also includes efficient card draw and selection like Faithful Mending and Ephemerate to dig for combo pieces. The deck has a flexible mana base with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent colors for spells. With a mix of reanimation, interaction, and card advantage, the deck aims to combo off and win the game quickly.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls. It also utilizes removal spells like Fatal Push and Thraben Charm to control the board and protect its creatures. The deck's aggressive strategy is further supported by cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment, which allow for additional damage to be dealt to the opponent. Overall, the key concept is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle while disrupting their game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to generate value and quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. It aims to establish a board presence using cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble, while also controlling the game with cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card draw and synergy with artifacts, while cards like Pinnacle Emissary and Weapons Manufacturing offer additional threats. Overall, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while utilizing its artifact synergy to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo potential of Broodscale Bloodchief and other creatures to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck focuses on using cards like Kozilek's Command and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate mana production and play big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. Boseiju, Who Endures and Blade of the Bloodchief provide additional support and protection for the combo pieces. The inclusion of Urza's Saga and Vexing Bauble helps to find key cards and maintain card advantage throughout the game.

Utilize Eldrazi creatures and ramp spells to control the board and overwhelm your opponent with powerful threats. Use Eldrazi Temple and ramp spells like Utopia Sprawl to quickly cast Emrakul, the Promised End and other Eldrazi creatures. Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Return provide board control while Sire of Seven Deaths and Sowing Mycospawn apply pressure to your opponent. Ugin, Eye of the Storms can help clear the board and protect your creatures, while Bojuka Bog disrupts graveyard strategies. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying massive Eldrazi threats.

The main concept of this deck is to focus on a combination of aggressive creature abilities and blinking effects to control the board and disrupt the opponent's plans. By utilizing cards like Flickerwisp, Ephemerate, and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to constantly generate value by resetting powerful creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to maintain tempo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, this deck aims to pressure the opponent while controlling the game with strategic blinking effects.

This aggro deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent by utilizing cheap and efficient creatures and spells. The key strategy involves disrupting the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Dragon's Rage Channeler. Unholy Heat and Moonshadow provide removal and additional reach, while cards like Vexing Bauble and Mishra's Bauble help with card selection. The land base is streamlined to ensure consistent mana sources for the deck's low-cost spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing instant spells to reanimate creatures from the graveyard, particularly Atraxa, Grand Unifier, in order to generate powerful combos and overwhelm the opponent. It also includes control elements like counterspells and removal to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game through a combination of powerful creature reanimation and disruptive spells.

The deck focuses on aggressive early game plays, utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to quickly pressure the opponent. It looks to gain card advantage through cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration, while also having the ability to pump up its creatures with cards like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge. The deck aims to apply constant pressure, using cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Slickshot Show-Off to push for early damage and close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck aims to utilize a combination of creature toolbox cards, tutoring abilities, and mana acceleration to quickly assemble powerful combos and overwhelm opponents. By relying on a mix of sacrifice outlets, recursion, and disruptive creatures, the deck can adapt to different situations and disrupt opponents' strategies. The inclusion of Chord of Calling allows for the deck to search for crucial creatures at instant speed, while cards like Young Wolf and Cauldron Familiar provide resilient threats that can be repeatedly brought back to the battlefield. Overall, this deck focuses on flexibility and synergy to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory.
